title: Is the zero-divisor graph Gamma(Z_43 x Z_25) prime?
generalization_signal: At p = 43 the fixed wrapper B = {1,19,23,29} is decisively obsolete because its spill already has size 27 > 20, but the moved sparse barrier B = {1,43,47,53} still leaves a small factor palette for A ∪ B and exactly 42 usable primes for C. This supports a movable sparse-barrier sufficient-condition theorem for Γ(Z_p × Z_25).
strongest_honest_claim: An explicit prime-labeling witness for Γ(Z_43 × Z_25) survives bounded rediscovery audit, direct checker rerun, and independent skeptical recomputation. This is still only feeder evidence, but it materially strengthens the live movable sparse-barrier companion line because the redesigned barrier B = {1,43,47,53} still works at p = 43 even though the old fixed wrapper is far past its spill boundary.
candidate_theorem_slice: A movable sparse-barrier sufficient-condition slice for Γ(Z_p × Z_25): if {1,...,5p+19} admits a pairwise-coprime barrier B = {1,q_1,q_2,q_3} whose nontrivial multiples fit in the 20 A-slots and still leaves a disjoint prime pool of size p - 1 for C, then the four-interface bridge theorem yields a prime labeling.
next_action: promote the verified p = 43 moved-barrier witness into the family dossier, then run generalize before testing z47-z25-prime-zero-divisor-graph; do not run Lean on this exact instance alone
